raw_output:
PR #128 review (Claude, Fable 5) — Group 2: Security

Low — --base option-injection into git argv, no untrusted reach: a value beginning with "-" is parsed by git as an option. In CI the value is always prefixed origin/<base_ref> so it cannot start with "-"; locally only the operator sets it. Hardening: reject leading "-".

Low — docs/reviews/ is a hash-exempt channel: a PR can rewrite PRIOR PRs' audit records without invalidating any attestation. Nothing under the path is executed or served; spec explicitly accepts non-genuine artifacts as out of scope — accepted residual.

Info — CI event model sound: plain pull_request (not pull_request_target); no secrets in the job; head-sha checkout is ordinary test-the-PR behavior; fork PRs get read-only token. No workflow-level permissions block — contents:read would be cheap hardening. Inherent limit: on pull_request the workflow file comes from the PR branch, so a PR can gut its own gate job while reporting green — human review of ci.yml diffs remains the control. Script reads only docs/reviews/pr-<int>/ (argparse type=int blocks traversal), writes nothing, no network.

Info — Threat model impact "none to register": agree. No app trust boundary (B1-B5) touched. Scope note: S603/S607 ignore is a blanket for all future scripts/** files.

findings.summary: --base option-injection into git argv (no untrusted reach in CI)
findings.reason: Leading-dash values rejected with SystemExit; test added.

findings.summary: docs/reviews/ is hash-exempt — prior PRs' audit records rewritable without tripping staleness
findings.reason: Accepted residual, consistent with the spec's guards-forgetting-not-deception scope; git history + human spot-checks protect audit records. Recorded in spec.

findings.summary: No workflow permissions block; checkout persists credentials
findings.reason: review-gate job now runs contents:read + persist-credentials:false; workflow-wide sweep tracked as #127.
